title: Freestanding GdBa2Cu3O7 Thin Films via Optimized Buffer Layer Design: Preserving Superconducting Properties
authors: Kazumasa Iida, Kai Walter, Takafumi Hatano, Kose Morinaga, Manuela Erbe, Hongye Gao, Satoshi Hata, Jens Hänisch
abstract: Freestanding GdBa2Cu3O7 (GdBCO) superconducting thin films were fabricated using a water-soluble Sr3Al2O6 (SAO) sacrificial layer in combination with thermal release tape. An amorphous Al2O3 capping layer was introduced to suppress crack formation during the lift-off process. The influence of buffer-layer design inserted between the GdBCO and SAO layers was systematically investigated with respect to structural integrity and superconducting properties after lift-off. A LaAlO3/SrTiO3 bilayer buffer was found to be essential for maintaining epitaxial growth and a superconducting transition temperature (Tc) of approximately 92 K after lift-off, comparable to that of the as-grown films. In contrast, a reversed SrTiO3/LaAlO3 bilayer and single-layer buffer structures led to a suppression of Tc, highlighting the critical role of stacking sequence. These results demonstrate that optimization of the buffer-layer design is a key factor for realizing high-quality freestanding GdBCO films while maintaining their superconducting characteristics.
summary: This paper demonstrates a method for creating high-quality freestanding GdBCO superconducting thin films by optimizing buffer-layer design. By using a specific LaAlO3/SrTiO3 bilayer, the researchers successfully preserved a high superconducting transition temperature of 92 K. This advancement is crucial for applications requiring flexible or freestanding high-Tc superconducting components.
main_problem: The challenge of fabricating high-quality freestanding GdBa2Cu3O7 (GdBCO) superconducting thin films without degrading their superconducting properties during the lift-off process.
main_result: A LaAlO3/SrTiO3 bilayer buffer layer was identified as essential for maintaining epitaxial growth and a superconducting transition temperature (Tc) of approximately 92 K after lift-off.
method: The researchers used a water-soluble Sr3Al2O6 (SAO) sacrificial layer with thermal release tape and an amorphous Al2O3 capping layer, systematically varying the buffer-layer design between the GdBCO and SAO.
model_or_system: The physical system consists of freestanding GdBCO superconducting thin films fabricated on a sacrificial Sr3Al2O6 layer with various buffer layer configurations (LaAlO3/SrTiO3, SrTiO3/LaAlO3, or single-layer structures).
key_observables: Superconducting transition temperature (Tc), structural integrity, and epitaxial growth quality.
important_parameters: The stacking sequence of the buffer layers and the presence of the Al2O3 capping layer.
paper_structure: The paper describes the fabrication process, the systematic investigation of different buffer-layer designs, and the comparison of superconducting properties (Tc) between as-grown and lifted-off films.
analysis_basis: abstract only; PDF text extraction failed or PDF unavailable
